languages.modeling_guidance: Prefer structured modules, explicit object maps, and framework conventions instead of free-form string branching.
languages.localization_guidance: Keep user-facing copy behind a resource, catalog, or message boundary suitable for the platform.
languages.configuration_guidance: Use the platform's standard configuration and environment layering instead of ad-hoc constants.

Model commands, routes, and events with typed abstractions native to the language/platform.
Externalize user-facing copy, status text, and errors behind a localization/resource strategy.
Keep configuration, secrets, and environment handling aligned with the platform's standard settings model.
Verify tests and docs cover the latest feature flow before promoting the next brief version.
Only promote a new brief version after the final output is runnable, tested, and safe to review or roll back.
Preserve the last known-good active version when a newer brief version still has unresolved gaps.
Do not create the next brief version until the current project version is fully implemented and has no open gaps.
If a brief still has gaps, close them in the same brief version instead of pushing them into a newer brief.
When the project supports Docker review, make the latest promoted version runnable in Docker before asking for review.
